candidate_problems.text: What specific criticism filters are sufficient to distinguish explanatory improvement from stylistic or benchmark-fitting improvement in a token-predictive system?

Can a system built from token prediction plus structured criticism generate explanations that outperform all training-set-adjacent recombinations on genuinely novel problems, rather than only appearing novel to human judges?
What forms of memory and recursive reuse actually change the search distribution toward rare explanatory configurations rather than merely reinforcing earlier mistakes or local optima?
Is there a principled boundary between guided exploration of explanatory possibility and very sophisticated interpolation, or does the distinction collapse under some architectures?
